codeReview.summary: Re-review confirms the prior blocking issue is still present: children_by_horizon does not match the feature/spec API contract. Other horizon query logic appears aligned with expected behavior.

issues.description: `children_by_horizon` is still implemented as `-> Option<Vec<&Node>>` and returns `None` for missing parent IDs, while the feature contract requires `children_by_horizon(parent_id) -> Vec<&Node>` (features.json description, research/horizon-spec.md Bead H8). Blocking contract mismatch remains unresolved.
`test_children_by_horizon_all_none` says it checks stable ordering, but it only asserts membership via `contains` and does not assert actual output order.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is an unresolved contract/convention mismatch around missing-parent semantics for horizon child queries: mission feature/spec requires `Vec`, but established tree APIs use `Option<Vec<_>>` and this implementation followed the existing convention instead of the feature contract.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description in mission features.json: `children_by_horizon(parent_id) -> Vec<&Node>`; spec in research/horizon-spec.md Bead H8: same; existing tree API in sd-core/src/tree.rs:231 uses `children(&self, id: &str) -> Option<Vec<&Node>>`; implementation at sd-core/src/tree.rs:431 also uses `Option<Vec<&Node>>`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill requires manual verification (including `cargo doc` and API/doc review), but the worker recorded no interactive checks while reporting `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill procedure `.factory/skills/rust-library-worker/SKILL.md` section `6. Manual Verification`; handoff for worker session b1e752f4-1bd8-4bf6-b87f-6457014818c7 shows `verification.interactiveChecks: []` and `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Fail: prior blocking API-signature mismatch remains. `children_by_horizon` still returns `Option<Vec<&Node>>` instead of the contracted `Vec<&Node>`, so the re-run does not clear the original failure.
